english: ₀drén bdúd-kyis byin-gyis brlabs ('dren bdud kyis byin gyis brlabs) heretical teachers sent and fitted out by the devil; so also Tar. 46, 13; to create, to change into Mil., Tar.; hence byin-rlabs (byin rlabs) blessing, byin-rlabs byéd-pa (byin rlabs byed pa), resp. mdzád-pa (mdzad pa) frq., gtóṅ-ba (gtong ba), resp. stsól-ba (stsol ba) Cs., = byin-gyis rláb-pa (byin gyis rlab pa); byin-rlabs-čan (byin rlabs can), byin-rlabs daṅ ldán-pa (byin rlabs dang ldan pa) blessed, sanctified, highly favoured, men or things Pth.; so also byin-rlabs żúgs-pa (byin rlabs zhugs pa) Mil.; ₀dre-₀dúl byin-rlabs ('dre 'dul byin rlabs) blessing pronounced against demons, exorcism of devils Mil.; meton.: I am the ₀págs-pai byin-rlabs ('phags pa'i byin rlabs) of all of you, he who will help you to go to heaven Glr.

Cs., the relics of a saint, or the place where they are kept ('depository of blessings'); also in the shape of pills, which liberal donors receive from their Lamas, and which they swallow, particularly in the hour of death.
1. sbst. calf of the leg, byin-pa ṅá-ba (byin pa nga ba) pain in the calf; byin-súl (byin sul) Cs. 'hollow on the inward side of the thigh'(?). -- II. pf. of sbyín-pa (sbyin pa).
Sch. All, the whole; general; byin-gyis ṗrá-ba (byin gyis phra ba) by degrees, more and more fine etc.?
v. byin (byin) 2.
pf. byibs (byibs) 1. to cover, to wrap up, gós-kyis (gos kyis) Lt. -- 2. Cs. to hide, conceal, keep secret, hush up.
1. Pur. *biu*, little bird, bird S.g. -- 2. Sch. alpine hare.
to stroke, mgó-bo-la byil-byil byéd-pa (mgo bo la byil byil byed pa) to stroke a person's head Pth.
naked Sch.

1. child, esp. little child; byís-(pai) nad (byis pa'i nad) disease of children Med.; byis-pa btsá-tabs (byis pa btsa' thabs) obstetric science Med.; byis-stón (byis ston) v. ṅá-zla (nga zla) sub ṅa (nga) I, 2; byis-pai blo (byis pa'i blo) Cs. childishness, want of judgment; byis-pai skyé-bo (byis pa'i skye bo) a plain, ignorant person, a person not initiated Thgy., S.O.; mo-byis (mo byis) girl, lass Mil.; byis-pa-zúṅ-żig (byis pa zung zhig) Cs., twins. -- 2. boy, lad, till about the age of 16 years, frq. (W. not in use).
= byi-ru (byi ru).

1. unguent, ointment, salve, whether as colouring-matter, medicine or sweet scent Dzl., Med.; byúg-pa ska (byug pa ska) thick ointment, thick plaster; byúg-pa sla (byug pa sla) thin unguent Cs. -- 2. foot-bath W., perh. better: bčúg-pa (bcug pa).
Lex. = gral (gral), place, in a certain succession or row; byug-ris żog (byug ris zhog) make room, leave a place empty Sch.
v. ₀byúg-pa ('byug pa); byugs-spos (byugs spos) anointing-oil Sch.

v. ₀byuṅ-ba ('byung ba); byuṅ-tsul (byung tshul) history, story, particulars of any event, ṅai byuṅ-tsul dé-ltar yda (nga'i byung tshul de ltar gda') that is my story Mil.; byuṅ-rábs (byung rabs) Sch. id.

B., esp. of later times and col., Ld. also byus (byus), misfortune, mishap, accident, byur čé-żiṅ ḃu mi ysós-na (byur che zhing bu mi gsos na) if one has the great misfortune not to be able to keep a child alive Dom.; mí-la byur ₀gél-ba (mi la byur 'gel ba) to draw down misfortune on a person Dom.; *ṅá-la ǰur čug soṅ (*nga la jur cug song) W., *ǰhur (*jhur) C., I have had misfortune, I have been unfortunate; byúr-gyi (byur gyi), also byúr-čan (byur can) unlucky, disastrous, perilous. -- *ǰhur-nág (*jhur nag) great calamity C. -- byur-sél (byur sel) preservative against misfortune. -- raṅ-byur-rdó (rang byur rdo) was explained: a sling-stone with which one hits one's self.
Cs. also -bu (-bu), vulg. byur-byúr (byur byur) heaped, a heaped measure of corn or meal; byúr-por bkaṅ (byur por bkang) Thgy.

1. = byeu (byi'u) little bird, bye-gliṅ (bye gling) bird's nest Ma.; bye-ṗrúg (bye phrug) a young little bird Dzl., also bya-ṗrúg (bya phrug); bye - brún (bye brun) bird-dung Wdn.; byeu úr-pa (byi'u 'ur pa) Sch. partridge. -- 2. v. byé-ma (bye ma).
1. bird's head. -- 2. an officinal mineral S.g.

ten million, byé-ba-ṗrag ysum daṅ sá-ya-ṗrag drug (bye ba phrag gsum dang sa ya phrag drug) thirty-six million; byé - ba sa - ya (bye ba sa ya), eleven million; it seems to be among the larger numbers one of the most popular, as the word million is in English.

1. difference, diversity, kó-bo daṅ saṅs-rgyás bye-brág ći yod (kho bo dang sangs rgyas bye brag ci yod) what difference is there between me and Buddha? Dzl.; bye-brág ₀byéd-pa (bye brag 'byed pa) to find,
